mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the past 3 months signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in payroll employment over the past 2 months signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts over the past 2 months signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production over the past 2 months signals a weakening supply chain that boosts corporate risk and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in payroll employment over the past 2 months signals a weakening labor market that boosts corporate risk and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in housing starts over the past 2 months signals a weakening housing market that boosts corporate risk and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening housing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
